name: web-sdk-baseline
description: Web SDK capability stub — the TypeScript SDK web apps consume to talk to Identity, Preferences, Notifications, and (since v3) Web Runtime. Per-version interfaces live in 02-v2.0.json and 03-v3.0.json. The bitemporal `current_version` history defined here makes time-travel queries return the correct shape for `?as_of=…`.

attributes.summary: TypeScript/JavaScript SDK that web apps consume to interact with the platform's Identity, Preferences, Notifications, and Web Runtime services. The default runtime SDK referenced by Web Runtime since v3.

facts.title: Overview
facts.body: The Web SDK is the canonical client library for browser apps building on the platform. It wraps Identity (auth + session), Preferences (per-user settings), Notifications (in-app inbox + send), and — since v3 — Web Runtime (zones, slots, menus, entitlements). Apps integrate by installing `@digital-platform/web-sdk` from npm and calling `init()` once at boot. The SDK handles token caching, refresh, and silent re-auth; consumers never see a raw JWT. Two major versions ship in this seed — v2.4.0 (in use until 2026-02-01) and v3.0.0 (current). The v2→v3 jump is a breaking change; see the migration guide fact under v3.
